threat_model: Chaos rehearsal and invariant fuzz evidence can accidentally become live runtime authority if dry-run records are conflated with execution receipts. The main risks are unapproved runtime dispatch, random live fuzzing, external effects, filesystem mutation, deployment mutation, production-readiness claims, missing rollback evidence, and terminal closure without incident handoff. The mitigation is a pair of non-executing schemas and a deterministic validator that deny execution, require evidence refs, retain blocked reasons, and bind rollback plus incident handoff obligations.

required_checks.check: authority and approval check confirms Phi_gov authorization remains absent and success claims remain denied
policy bypass check confirms dry-run mode cannot become deployment or production-readiness authority
deployment witness check confirms chaos and fuzz reports do not dispatch runtime work or mutate deployment state
filesystem mutation check confirms invariant fuzzing stays deterministic fixture only and performs no file writes
path traversal check confirms report validation only reads fixed workspace-local schema and example paths
required evidence and blocked reason refs are retained
receipt integrity check confirms receipt refs and summary counts cannot drift
rollback and incident handoff obligations remain present before live rehearsal
audit visibility check confirms proof coverage matrix includes both resilience report surfaces

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode reports block runtime execution, random generation, external effects, filesystem mutation, deployment mutation, production-readiness claims, terminal closure, and success claims until named live evidence exists.

mitigations: keep resilience rehearsal report validation non-live and deterministic
require UAO and Phi_gov refs before runtime rehearsal authority can be reconsidered
require operator approval before live chaos or fuzz execution
require deterministic seed manifest before invariant fuzzing
require rollback and incident handoff evidence before terminal closure
reject deployment mutation, production-readiness claims, and success claims in Foundation Mode
